meta.title: F23 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 2 Series F23 convertible engine — 218i, 220i, 230i, M235i, M240i. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and N20 vs B-series. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series Convertible engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F23 engine typically costs £1,800–£8,000 supply only, depending on engine — the B38 1.5-litre sits at £1,800–£3,200, while the B58 M240i runs £4,800–£8,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,040. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which F23 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 2.0-litre (220i/230i) is the most dependable four-cylinder — a significant improvement over the N20 it replaced. The B58 six-cylinder is also highly reliable. Avoid early N20 engines unless timing chain work has been done.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the F23?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide wear on N20-powered cars (2015–2016), cooling system leaks on B-series engines, and EGR cooler failures on B47 diesels. All are well-understood and repairable.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW F23 reliable overall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — but it depends on the engine. The B-series engines (B38, B48, B58) are BMW's most reliable modern family. The N20 is the weak point — if you're buying an early F23, budget for timing chain inspection or replacement.

mainEntity.name: Which F23 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No outright avoidance — but a 2015–2016 N20-powered 220i or 228i without documented timing chain history deserves a thorough inspection. The B48 and B58 are the safer choices.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F23 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle (N20 timing chain), coolant loss or overheating (B-series cooling system), or white smoke and coolant loss (B47 EGR cooler) are key warnings. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an F23 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £10,000+ F23, a £3,200 B48 recon is 32% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £7,000 example, the same recon is 46% — still within our 70% BMW threshold. The F23 holds value well; replacement is almost always viable.

mainEntity.name: How long do F23 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: B-series engines (B38, B48, B58) can exceed 150,000 miles with proper maintenance. N20 engines can also reach 150,000+ miles but often need timing chain work before that. B47 diesels are capable of 150,000+ miles with EGR cooler attention.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F23 and the F22?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F22 is the 2 Series Coupé (2014–2021), while the F23 is the Convertible (2015–2021). Both share the same platforms and engine ranges, but the F23 has additional structural reinforcement and a power-operated soft top. Maintenance costs are broadly similar, though labour access can vary slightly.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my F23 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, or the engine block — 1.5-litre three-cylinder is B38B15, 2.0-litre four-cylinder N20 or B48B20, 3.0-litre six-cylinder N55B30 or B58B30, diesel is B47D20A. Tell us your variant and registration year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW 2 Series F23 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F23 Convertible — from the 1.5-litre three-cylinder 218i to the 2.0-litre four-cylinders and the 3.0-litre six-cylinder M240i. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the engine changes that defined the F23's short life. Backed by 147+ real UK F23 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 2 Series F23 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F23 convertible specifically — from the base 218i to the M240i, including the N20-to-B48 and N55-to-B58 changes that came with the 2016 model-year update.

overview.intro: The F23 is the convertible version of BMW's first-generation 2 Series, launched in January 2015 as the successor to the E88 1 Series Convertible. Built alongside the F22 coupé at BMW's Leipzig plant, the F23 was a four-seat convertible with a power-operated soft top. The engine range launched with 2.0-litre N20 four-cylinders (220i, 228i) and the 3.0-litre N55 six-cylinder (M235i), plus B47 diesels. A significant July 2016 update replaced the N20 and N55 with the newer B48 and B58 modular engines, and the 1.5-litre B38 three-cylinder arrived in 2017 for the 218i. The F23 was produced through 2021.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW F22/F23 (rear-wheel drive) • Predecessor: E88 1 Series Convertible • Successor: G42 2 Series Convertible • Sibling body styles in this generation: F22 Coupé, F87 M2.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F23 convertible generated 147 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 2.0-litre petrol engines as these stylish convertibles enter the higher-mileage phase of their ownership cycle.

items.quote: The B48 is a significant improvement over the N20 — better cooling, more reliable timing chain design, and smoother delivery. The 230i is the sweet spot of the F23 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want modern turbo four-cylinder performance without the N20's concerns.

items.quote: Early F23s use the N20 engine — known for timing chain guide wear and VANOS issues. The B48 that replaced it from mid-2016 is a much safer bet.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who are prepared for timing chain maintenance or want a cheaper entry point.

items.quote: The N55 is robust but complex — VANOS, valvetronic and turbocharger repairs can be costly. The B58 that replaced it is a more reliable choice.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the first-generation M Performance convertible and are ready for the bills.

items.quote: The B48 is reliable, efficient and well-supported. A £2,800–£4,500 recon makes sense against a £10,000–£20,000 vehicle.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most cost-effective F23 ownership.

items.quote: The 1.5-litre three-cylinder is often overlooked by buyers chasing bigger engines — but it's a genuinely charming unit, reliable and surprisingly perky in the F23's lightweight chassis.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want low running costs and don't need six-cylinder pace.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Watch Closely — chain is the weak point
Best Petrol — reliable B48, chain improved
Best Six — B58 is a modern classic

economicsRule.title: The F23 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,200–£5,000 B48 recon against a £10,000–£20,000 F23 sits at 16–50% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even against a sub-£10,000 example, the same recon is 32–50% — a repair decision, not a financial one. The F23 holds value well enough that engine replacement is almost always viable.

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Guide Wear
problems.rootCause: The N20's timing chain guides are a known weak point — they can become brittle and fail, leading to chain stretch or catastrophic engine failure. Cold-start rattle is the classic early warning. The B48 that replaced it from July 2016 has an improved chain design.
problems.recommendation: If your N20-powered F23 has no documented timing chain history, treat it as a priority inspection — £800–£1,800 now is far cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows a failure. Cold-start rattle is the warning sign to watch for.

cta.label: Compare N20 engine prices for your F23 →

problems.title: B38/B48/B58 Cooling System
problems.rootCause: The B-series modular engines are generally reliable, but cooling system components — water pump, expansion tank, plastic fittings — can develop leaks. This is the most common B-series failure point.
problems.recommendation: On any B-series F23 approaching 60,000 miles, have the cooling system inspected — a £400–£900 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,800+ replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your F23 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler
problems.rootCause: The B47 diesel's EGR cooler can develop internal leaks, allowing coolant into the exhaust. This is a known issue across BMW's B47 engine family. The B47 is otherwise a significant improvement over the N47 — timing chain issues are far less common.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of coolant loss or white smoke, get the EGR cooler inspected — a £400–£900 repair is cheaper than the £3,200+ replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your F23 →

replacementCosts.h2: F23 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Engine Page baseline — no dedicated Engine Pages exist for these families yet, so baseline figures are drawn from the EM_Proprietary_Data Table A. Labour hours estimated at 8–12 hours for the three-cylinder B38, 10–14 hours for four-cylinder engines, and 12–16 hours for six-cylinder engines due to engine bay packaging.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F23 convertible only. The 228i and M235i were produced 2015–2016; the 230i and M240i replaced them from July 2016 production. The 218i (B38) joined the range in 2017. The 225d is the twin-turbo diesel.
